note: Mathlib/NumberTheory/Cyclotomic/Basic.lean:655 - `def CyclotomicField : Type w := (cyclotomic n K).SplittingField`, the n-th cyclotomic field over K as splitting field of the n-th cyclotomic polynomial; IsCyclotomicExtension instance attached.
verify_note: decl exists at cited line verbatim; QID confirmed 'cyclotomic field' (WD: field ext. of Q by primitive root of unity) via wikidata.org; existing WikiLean annotation edges (nodes Q11563, Q190109) already cite this same decl with status 'formalized' for this concept, corroborating exact over the K-generality.

note: Mathlib/Analysis/Normed/Affine/Simplex.lean:83 - `def Equilateral (s : Simplex R P n) : Prop := ∃ r, ∀ i j, i ≠ j → dist (s.points i) (s.points j) = r`, stated for a general n-simplex; Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/AffineSpace/Simplex/Basic.lean:53 defines `abbrev Triangle := Simplex k P 2`, so an equilateral triangle is exactly `(t : Triangle k P).Equilateral` at n=2 - a special case of the general-n predicate.
verify_note: both cited lines verbatim-verified (Equilateral def, Triangle abbrev at n=2); QID confirmed 'equilateral triangle' via wikidata.org; decl genuinely broader (arbitrary n) than the n=2 concept, so 'generalization' (not 'exact') is the honest match_kind, matching this project's established convention for parametrized-generality cases (cf. Tendsto/limit-of-sequence precedent).

note: Mathlib/Combinatorics/SimpleGraph/Walk/Chord.lean:45 - `def IsChordless (p : G.Walk u v) : Prop := ∀ e, ¬ p.IsChord e` (no edge of G joins two non-consecutive support vertices); Mathlib/Combinatorics/SimpleGraph/Connectivity/Subgraph.lean:370 proves `isInduced_toSubgraph : w.toSubgraph.IsInduced ↔ w.IsChordless`, tying chordlessness directly to induced-subgraph-hood. Stated for general walks, not restricted to `Path`s - an induced path is the conjunction with `Walk.IsPath`.
verify_note: both cited lines and the isInduced_toSubgraph iff verbatim-verified; QID confirmed 'induced path' via wikidata.org; no InducedPath/IsInducedPath decl exists anywhere in Mathlib's SimpleGraph tree, so IsChordless (applies to general walks, requires conjoining IsPath for the concept) is the correct best-available target and 'generalization' honestly describes it, not 'exact'.
